entities.name: LEGUME
entities.description: A group of plants for which improved seed varieties are developed for smallholder farmers

entities.name: SMALLHOLDER FARMERS
entities.description: Farmers in developing countries who rely on non-improved and improved seed varieties

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: A region where smallholder farmers heavily rely on non-improved and auto-saved variety seeds

entities.name: SOUTH ASIA
entities.description: A region where smallholder farmers have a relatively higher rate of improved seed use, up to 70%

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: A region showing the lowest rate of improved seed use, below 20%

entities.name: NON-IMPROVED SEED
entities.description: Auto-saved variety seeds that account for about 80% of planting material for many smallholder farmers

entities.name: IMPROVED SEED
entities.description: Seeds developed through breeding programs to meet specific demands and preferences

entities.name: BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Programs that create breakthroughs in crop varieties and develop new traits

entities.name: TRAITS
entities.description: Characteristics of recently developed crop varieties targeted to consumers and farmers

entities.name: CONSUMER DEMANDS
entities.description: Market requirements that influence the development of traits in new crop varieties

entities.name: FARMER PREFERENCES
entities.description: Preferences of farmers that influence the development of traits in new crop varieties
